summary: Deprecated audit trail for the F1 Help binary rename. The app disables F1 Help by renaming HelpPane.exe, but this is not an official policy surface.

setting.name: HelpPane Binary Rename
setting.casual_explanation: Pressing F1 in some old Windows contexts can open HelpPane. This tweak blocks that by renaming the executable.
setting.why_people_change_it: People do this when they never use legacy F1 help and want to stop accidental help popups.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: It is effective as a blunt tool, but it is more invasive than a policy or preference change.
setting.professional_notes: The provider renames C:\Windows\System32\HelpPane.exe to HelpPane.exe.disabled. That is tracked as an observed implementation detail rather than an official Microsoft management surface.

allowed_values.label: Default file state
allowed_values.meaning: HelpPane.exe is present at its normal path.

allowed_values.label: Renamed by app
allowed_values.meaning: The current app renames the file to a .disabled name.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app renames HelpPane.exe to HelpPane.exe.disabled. The upstream nohuto mirror documents the same HelpPane rename behavior and F1 handling.

states.rationale: Treat the normal file location as the baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: People intentionally reproducing the app's file-rename behavior

states.rationale: Keep the binary in place unless there is a strong reason to rename it.

recommended_profiles.bad_when: You explicitly want to suppress legacy F1 Help by force.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app file rename

states.rationale: Track the file-rename behavior the product applies.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting the current implementation.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a policy-backed or less invasive control surface.
tradeoffs: Binary rename is more invasive than most settings.

evidence.title: Current privacy provider file rename
evidence.summary: The provider renames C:\Windows\System32\HelpPane.exe to HelpPane.exe.disabled.

decision.why: The app implementation is a direct file rename, and the nohuto mirror documents the same HelpPane rename behavior and F1 handling. This is a deprecated audit trail rather than a policy surface, so no further VM validation is required for the record itself.
blocking_issues: Binary rename is more invasive than a validated policy or preference control.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Works via renaming HelpPane.exe (Help and Support Windows desktop application) which was the help component in W8/W8.1. Note that HelpPane still handles the F1 shortcut.
validation_proof.notes: Backfilled from nohuto mirror evidence. The same HelpPane rename behavior is described upstream in the mirror, and the current app implementation still renames C:\Windows\System32\HelpPane.exe to HelpPane.exe.disabled. This remains a deprecated audit trail rather than an official policy surface.
